Here are the two FHIR queries to run and then union on `subject` (patient) IDs:

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|203483005&_elements=subject
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|8640&_elements=subject
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| # | Resource | Code System | Code | Meaning |
|---|----------|-------------|------|---------|
| 1 | `Condition` | SNOMED CT | `203483005` | Drug-induced avascular necrosis (AVN) |
| 2 | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm | `8640` | Prednisone |

---

### Union Logic (Post-Processing)

After retrieving results:

```
patientSet1 = { entry.resource.subject.reference for entry in Condition results }
patientSet2 = { entry.resource.subject.reference for entry in MedicationRequest results }

unionPatients = patientSet1 ∪ patientSet2
```

> **Note:** `_elements=subject` is included to minimize payload — only the `subject` (patient reference) field is returned per resource, making the union step efficient. Remove it if full resource data is needed.

---

### Optional Follow-up

To fetch full `Patient` resources for the union cohort:

```
Patient?_id=<id1>,<id2>,<id3>,...
```
